title: 10 best herbal creams for overeating

remedy_properties: Digestive Enzymes: Ginger contains digestive enzymes that help break down food and reduce bloating and discomfort after eating, making it easier to stop when satisfied.

Anti-Inflammatory Compounds: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, can help reduce inflammation in the gut and alleviate symptoms of indigestion and nausea, leading to a decrease in overeating.

Serotonin Regulation: Ginger has been shown to affect serotonin levels in the brain, which can help regulate appetite and food intake by reducing cravings for unhealthy foods and increasing feelings of fullness.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with overeating because it contains compounds that aid digestion and reduce nausea. The anti-inflammatory properties of ginger can help alleviate symptoms of indigestion and bloating, making it easier to manage overeating episodes. By reducing discomfort and promoting digestive balance, ginger creams may help individuals develop healthier eating habits and reduce the likelihood of overindulgence. This natural remedy can also help regulate appetite and support overall gut health.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These phenolic compounds have been shown to reduce appetite and improve digestion, which can help individuals manage overeating by reducing hunger and promoting a feeling of fullness.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols have anti-inflammatory and digestive-aiding properties that can help alleviate symptoms associated with overeating, such as bloating and discomfort.

Zingiberene: This sesquiterpene has been found to have a regulatory effect on appetite and metabolism, potentially helping individuals with overeating by promoting a balance between hunger and satiety.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used because they contain high levels of gingerols, which have natural anti-inflammatory properties that can aid in digestion and alleviate symptoms of overeating.
Roots: Roots are used due to their high concentration of zingibain, an enzyme that helps break down proteins and aid in digestion, reducing discomfort and nausea associated with overeating.
Leaves: Leaves are used for their calming properties, which can help soothe the stomach and reduce symptoms of overeating, such as bloating and nausea.
remedy_recipe: Extract 50 grams of dried zingiber officinale rhizomes and grind them into a fine powder.
Combine 25 grams of the powdered zingiber officinale with 100 grams of a carrier oil in a clean glass jar.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 40 degrees celsius for 30 minutes to facilitate infusion.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another clean glass jar and discard the solids.
Allow the infused oil to cool and then whip it with a hand mixer to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Curcumin: Turmeric's active compound, curcumin, has potent anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce cravings for comfort foods by reducing inflammation in the body, which is often linked to emotional eating.

Circulatory Stimulant Properties: Turmeric's circulatory stimulant properties can help improve blood circulation, which may aid in reducing symptoms of bloating and discomfort associated with overeating, making it easier to feel more balanced and in control.

Digestive Soothing Properties: Turmeric has natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can soothe digestive issues such as indigestion, cramps, and gas, often triggered by overeating, helping to reduce discomfort and promote a sense of calm.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, creams helps with overeating because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties and ability to regulate hunger hormones. The curcumin in Curcuma longa has been shown to reduce inflammation in the digestive system, which can lead to overeating by causing discomfort and pain. Additionally, Curcuma longa creams can help to reduce ghrelin levels, the hormone responsible for stimulating appetite, and increase leptin levels, the hormone that suppresses appetite, promoting a healthier relationship with food.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin helps reduce overeating by inhibiting the activity of enzymes involved in fat metabolism, thereby reducing the formation of new fat cells and promoting weight loss.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC helps reduce overeating by modulating the gut microbiome, reducing inflammation, and improving insulin sensitivity, which can lead to a decrease in appetite and weight gain.
Turmerone (alpha- and beta-): Turmerone helps reduce overeating by activating certain brain receptors that regulate appetite and satiety, leading to a decrease in food intake and weight gain.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The part primarily used to make creams for overeating is the Rhyzomes, as they contain the highest concentration of curcuminoids, which have anti-inflammatory and digestive benefits.
Roots: The Roots are also used, as they contain curcumin, which helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate digestive discomfort associated with overeating.
Leaves: The Leaves are another part used, as they contain volatile oils and flavonoids that may help to soothe digestive issues and reduce the symptoms of overeating.
remedy_recipe: Grate 100 grams of fresh curcuma longa rhizomes into small pieces using a fine grater.
Mix 200 milliliters of distilled water and 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Add the grated curcuma longa mixture to the beeswax mixture and stir well for 10 minutes.
Remove the double boiler from heat and let the mixture cool and thicken for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl and discard the solids.

remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant: Peppermint's menthol content helps to stimulate digestive enzymes, reducing inflammation and improving digestion, which can alleviate symptoms of bloating and discomfort associated with overeating.

Carminative and Antispasmodic: The essential oils in peppermint cream have a carminative effect, helping to release trapped gas and reduce the risk of constipation, while its antispasmodic properties ease abdominal cramps and spasms that may occur after overindulgence.

Mood-Boosting Antioxidant: Peppermint's antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress and promote a sense of calm, which can mitigate emotional eating triggers and reduce the desire to overeat due to stress or anxiety.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, creams helps with overeating because of its ability to boost digestion and reduce inflammation. The menthol in peppermint creams stimulates the digestive system, increasing the production of digestive enzymes and improving nutrient absorption. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties help soothe the stomach, reducing bloating and discomfort associated with overeating. This can help individuals feel more comfortable and in control after a large meal, promoting healthier eating habits and a more balanced lifestyle.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol can help reduce feelings of nausea and discomfort, which may lead to overeating, by numbing the stomach and promoting relaxation.
Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has been shown to have a satiety-inducing effect by stimulating the release of hormones that signal fullness and reduce appetite.
Caryophyllene: This sesquiterpene can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for overeating, by promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain menthol, which helps to reduce inflammation and ease digestive discomfort caused by overeating.
Stems: They are also a source of menthol, which helps to calm the digestive system and reduce nausea and bloating associated with overeating.
Roots: They contain menthone, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm the digestive system after overeating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh mentha x piperita leaves at peak oil production time for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer at room temperature for 7-10 days.
Steam distill the dried leaves in a ratio of 1 part plant to 4 parts water for 3 hours.
Mix the extracted oil with a base ingredient such as beeswax in a 10-20 ratio for 30 minutes.
Whip the mixture into a smooth cream using an electric mixer for 5-7 minutes.

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound helps to reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to cravings for unhealthy foods and lead to overeating. By reducing inflammation, glycyrrhizin can help regulate appetite and promote a healthy relationship with food.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in licorice cream have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce stress and anxiety that may trigger overeating. Flavonoids also have a calming effect on the nervous system, making it easier to make healthy choices.

Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids in licorice cream have been found to have anti-obesity properties by inhibiting the absorption of dietary fats and reducing body fat accumulation. This can help individuals who struggle with overeating and weight gain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with overeating because of its unique properties that promote satiety and appetite control. The glycyrrhizin in licorice root has been shown to slow down gastric emptying, which helps to reduce feelings of hunger and increase feelings of fullness. Additionally, licorice creams have been found to have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing stress-related eating behaviors and promoting a healthier relationship with food. This results in a reduced likelihood of overeating.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These triterpenoid glycosides may help with overeating by reducing cortisol levels and alleviating stress-related overeating.
Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin may assist in managing overeating by regulating appetite and metabolism, as well as providing anti-inflammatory effects that contribute to weight management.

Flavonoids: These phenolic compounds, such as quercetin and kaempferol, may help with overeating by modulating gut microbiota and improving glucose metabolism, which can reduce the likelihood of overeating.
remedy_parts: Roots: Glycyrrhizin, a compound found in the roots, can help reduce water retention and alleviate symptoms associated with overeating.
Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain various flavonoids and saponins, which can aid in digestion and help alleviate symptoms of overeating.
Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain glycyrrhizin and other compounds that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with overeating.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 10 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and crush them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powder with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of emulsifying wax in a heat-proof mixing bowl.
Melt the mixture in a double boiler at 160-180 degrees fahrenheit for 10-15 minutes with gentle stirring.
Add 20 milliliters of distilled water and 5 milliliters of glycerin to the melted mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into sterile containers and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anxiolytic properties: Chamomile cream helps reduce anxiety, which can contribute to emotional eating and overeating. By calming the nervous system, it reduces cravings for comfort foods.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory effects may help alleviate digestive discomfort and bloating associated with overindulgence, reducing the likelihood of further overeating.

Digestive soothing properties: Chamomile contains apigenin, an flavonoid that helps relax smooth muscle in the digestive tract, easing cramps, spasms, and discomfort, making it easier to stick to a healthy diet.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, creams helps with overeating because of its ability to soothe digestive issues and promote relaxation. The anti-inflammatory properties in chamomile can calm digestive discomfort, such as bloating and cramps, associated with overeating. Additionally, chamomile's calming effects can help reduce stress and anxiety, which often contribute to overeating behaviors. By promoting a sense of calm and relaxation, chamomile creams can aid in developing healthier eating habits and reducing the urge to overindulge. This natural approach can lead to a more balanced relationship with food.
remedy_constituents: Apinene: An anti-inflammatory terpene that reduces stress and anxiety, which can contribute to overeating, by promoting relaxation and reducing emotional triggers for overconsumption.
Apigenin: A flavonoid phenolic compound that has been shown to have anxiolytic and anti-stress effects, potentially reducing the urge to overeat by modulating the body's response to stress.

Chamazulene: A sesquiterpene lactone with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with overeating, promoting a healthier relationship with food.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their calming and soothing properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of overeating.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids and other compounds that can aid in digestion and reduce inflammation caused by overeating.
Seeds: They are used for their potential to help reduce water retention and alleviate symptoms of indigestion and bloating associated with overeating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of matricaria chamomilla flowers in the early morning when they are fully open.
Dry the flowers in a well-ventilated area at room temperature for 7-10 days to prevent spoilage.
Infuse 30 grams of dried flowers in 200 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container to remove the solids.
Add 10 milliliters of beeswax and 20 milliliters of coconut oil to the infused oil mixture and heat at 60 degrees celsius for 10 minutes.

remedy_properties: Choleretic properties: Dandelion cream's ability to stimulate bile production can help improve digestion and reduce symptoms of indigestion and bloating, making it easier to manage portions and avoid overeating.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The anti-inflammatory compounds in dandelion cream may help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to cravings and overeating, while also soothing digestive discomfort.
Laxative properties: Dandelion's natural laxative effects can help regulate bowel movements and prevent constipation, reducing the likelihood of overeating due to feelings of fullness or discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, creams helps with overeating because it contains compounds that support digestive health and reduce bloating. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties can soothe the stomach and alleviate discomfort associated with overindulgence. Additionally, dandelion's diuretic properties can help eliminate excess water retention, reducing the appearance of water weight. As a result, individuals who use Taraxacum officinale creams may experience a decrease in food cravings and an overall sense of well-being.
remedy_constituents: Taraxasterol: This triterpenoid saponin may help with overeating by regulating appetite and improving digestion, potentially leading to reduced food intake.
Inulin: A type of fructan, inulin may help with overeating by promoting feelings of fullness and satiety, reducing the likelihood of overeating.
Chrysophanic acid: A phenolic compound, chrysophanic acid may help with overeating by inhibiting the growth of gut bacteria that contribute to weight gain and metabolic disorders.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used for their diuretic and laxative properties.
Roots: They are used for their bitter compounds that can stimulate digestion.
Flowers: They are used for their anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 100g of fresh dandelion flowers and leaves in 500ml of carrier oil for 2 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding the solids.
Melt 100g of beeswax and 50g of shea butter in a double boiler over low heat.
Combine the infused oil with the melted beeswax and shea butter mixture stirring until well combined.
Pour the mixture into small tin containers and allow it to cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Emulsifying properties: Slippery elm contains mucilages that form a protective barrier on the digestive tract, helping to soothe and calm inflammation and irritation, which can reduce cravings for comfort foods.

Gastroprotective effects: The herb's anti-inflammatory compounds help to protect the stomach lining from damage caused by overeating or eating spicy or acidic foods, reducing discomfort and nausea that may lead to further overindulgence.
Satiety-promoting fiber: Slippery elm is a natural source of soluble fiber, which can help slow down digestion and absorption of food, leading to feelings of fullness and reduced appetite.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ulmus rubra, also known as slippery elm, creams helps with overeating because of its soothing and calming properties that ease digestive discomfort. It forms a protective barrier in the gut, reducing inflammation and irritation that can lead to overeating. The cream's emollient properties also help to calm the stomach, making it easier to manage food cravings and portion sizes. By promoting a healthy digestive environment, Ulmus rubra cream can help regulate eating habits and support a balanced diet.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: These compounds, including ellagic acid and gallic acid, may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to overeating by promoting feelings of discomfort and lethargy.

Triterpenoids (oleanolic acid and ursolic acid): These compounds have been shown to have appetite-suppressing and weight-loss-promoting effects by inhibiting the production of digestive enzymes and reducing inflammation in the gut.

Flavonoids (quercetin and kaempferol): These antioxidants may help regulate appetite and metabolism by influencing the gut-brain axis, reducing inflammation, and improving glucose metabolism, potentially leading to reduced overeating and weight management.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to make teas and tinctures for digestive issues and to soothe the mucous membranes.
Leaves: The leaves are not commonly used in herbal medicine, but the inner bark is the most commonly used part.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of ulmus rubra bark, dried for 2 weeks, to create a medicinal base.
Combine 2 parts of dried ulmus rubra bark with 1 part of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 20-25 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax melts.
Add 1 part of coconut oil and 1 part of shea butter to the mixture, stirring constantly.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool for 30-40 minutes before whipping into a creamy texture.

remedy_properties: Demulcent property: Marshmallow root extracts form a soothing film on the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by excessive food consumption, which can lead to discomfort and overeating.

Anti-inflammatory property: Herbal marshmallow creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and polyphenols that help reduce swelling and inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of indigestion and discomfort associated with overeating.

Soothing mucilages: The mucilage-rich extracts in herbal marshmallow creams coat the digestive tract, calming digestive issues caused by overconsumption of food, such as bloating, gas, and stomach pain, making it easier to stop eating.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Althaea officinalis, also known as marshmallow, creams helps with overeating because it has a soothing and calming effect on the digestive system. The mucilaginous properties of marshmallow root coat and protect the mucous membranes in the stomach and intestines, reducing inflammation and irritation. This can lead to a decrease in bloating and discomfort associated with overeating, making it easier to manage portion sizes and develop healthier eating habits. Regular use of Althaea officinalis creams may also promote a sense of fullness and satisfaction.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages in Althaea officinalis creams help with overeating by forming a protective barrier in the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and irritation that can lead to excessive eating.

Triterpenoid saponins: Triterpenoid saponins in Althaea officinalis creams aid in reducing overeating by regulating appetite and improving digestion, thereby promoting a feeling of fullness and satisfaction.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in Althaea officinalis creams help with overeating by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the digestive system, which can contribute to overeating behaviors.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Althaea officinalis are used to create creams due to their high mucilage content, which helps soothe and calm digestive issues.
Leaves: The leaves of Althaea officinalis are used in creams because they contain anti-inflammatory properties that can aid in reducing swelling and discomfort associated with overeating.
Barks: The barks of Althaea officinalis are used in creams for their soothing and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate digestive discomfort and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 ounce of dried marshmallow roots and clean them thoroughly to remove any debris.
Combine the cleaned roots with 2 cups of distilled water in a saucepan and heat it gently over low heat.
Reduce the heat and simmer the mixture for 30 minutes to extract the roots' active properties.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a separate container and discard the solids immediately.
Mix the infused liquid with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter in a double boiler.

remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant Properties: Fennel cream's digestive stimulant properties help stimulate digestion, reduce bloating and gas, and alleviate symptoms associated with indigestion, which can make it easier to stop overeating.

Carminative Properties: The carminative properties of fennel cream help eliminate trapped air from the digestive system, reducing discomfort and spasms that may lead to overeating due to feelings of fullness or discomfort.

Satiety-Inducing Compounds: Fennel cream contains compounds such as anethole, which have been shown to increase feelings of fullness and reduce hunger cravings, making it easier to stick to a healthy diet and avoid overeating.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare, also known as fennel, creams helps with overeating because of its natural ability to regulate appetite and digestive processes. The active compounds present in fennel, such as anethole and fenchone, have been shown to stimulate digestion and reduce hunger pangs. By alleviating symptoms of bloating and discomfort, fennel creams promote a feeling of fullness and satisfaction, making it easier to manage portion sizes and maintain a balanced diet. This natural remedy also supports overall gut health, leading to a healthier relationship with food.
remedy_constituents: Anethole: Anethole, a terpene compound, helps to reduce appetite by slowing down gastric emptying and increasing feelings of fullness, thereby reducing overeating.

Foeniculin: Foeniculin, a phenolic compound, has been shown to have a satiety-inducing effect by reducing the expression of genes involved in appetite regulation, leading to a decrease in food intake.

Trans-anethole: Trans-anethole, another terpene compound found in Fennel, has been found to have a potential role in reducing food intake and body weight by inhibiting the release of ghrelin, a hormone that stimulates appetite.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Used to make creams due to their carminative properties, which help alleviate digestive issues.
Leaves: Used to make creams because of their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help soothe the digestive system.
Essential oil from the seeds: Used to make creams due to its carminative and antispasmodic properties, which help to ease digestive discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/2 cup of fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers in the morning when they are at their peak potency.
Dry the harvested foeniculum vulgare in a single layer at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours to remove excess moisture.
Grind 2 tablespoons of dried foeniculum vulgare into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax with 1 teaspoon of foeniculum vulgare powder in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture continuously for 5 minutes and then remove it from heat after it reaches 160 degrees fahrenheit.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba contains antioxidants that can help protect the body against free radicals and oxidative stress, which may contribute to various health issues.

Vascular benefits: The plant's extracts have been shown to improve blood flow and circulation, which may be beneficial for people who experience digestive issues or other vascular-related problems that may lead to overeating.
Anxiolytic properties: Some studies suggest that Ginkgo biloba may have anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects, which could potentially help individuals manage emotional eating habits.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, creams helps with overeating because of its potential to enhance blood circulation and brain function. The flavonoids and terpenoids in Ginkgo biloba may improve memory, concentration, and mood, reducing the likelihood of overeating due to emotional factors. Additionally, improved circulation may help regulate appetite and metabolism, leading to a healthier relationship with food. As a result, individuals who use Ginkgo biloba creams may experience a decrease in overeating habits and an overall improvement in their eating behavior.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds may help regulate appetite and reduce overeating by inhibiting the activity of certain enzymes involved in the breakdown and absorption of glucose.
Terpenoids (specifically, ginkgolides): These compounds have been shown to have appetite-suppressing properties, possibly by influencing the brain's reward centers and reducing cravings for food.

Bilobalide: This terpenoid may help regulate appetite and reduce overeating by modulating the activity of certain neurotransmitters, such as serotonin and dopamine, which play a role in appetite regulation and satiety.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Ginkgo biloba due to their high flavonoid content, which helps in improving circulation and reducing inflammation associated with overeating.
Barks: The barks of Ginkgo biloba contain flavonoids and terpenoids that help in reducing inflammation and improving circulation, making it useful in creams for overeating.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds contain flavonoids and terpenoids that help in improving circulation and reducing inflammation, making them a popular choice for creams related to overeating.
remedy_recipe: Extract 120 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves from a reputable supplier and chop them finely for infusion.
Combine chopped ginkgo biloba leaves with 2 liters of carrier oil in a clean glass jar and seal.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily, to facilitate infusion.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax with 10 grams of vitamin e oil and 100 ml of the infused ginkgo biloba oil in a double boiler.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bitter orange creams helps with overeating because they contain natural ingredients that stimulate digestion and curb hunger cravings. The bitter compounds in the cream, such as naringenin, help to slow down gastric emptying, keeping you feeling fuller for longer and reducing the likelihood of snacking between meals. Additionally, the cream's citrus flavor can also aid in appetite suppression, making it a helpful tool for those looking to manage their food intake and maintain a healthy weight.
remedy_properties: Citrus flavonoids: These compounds have been shown to reduce food cravings and improve weight management by suppressing appetite and increasing feelings of fullness, making it easier to stick to a healthy diet.

Vitamin C: This essential nutrient plays a crucial role in the regulation of blood sugar levels and insulin sensitivity, which can help reduce carbohydrate cravings and stabilize energy levels, reducing the likelihood of overeating.

Peel extracts: The peel of bitter oranges contains compounds that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce stress and anxiety-related eating behaviors, making it easier to maintain a healthy appetite.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in alkaloids, bitter orange roots help reduce appetite and alleviate symptoms of overeating by stimulating digestion and reducing inflammation.
Buds: Containing volatile oils, bitter orange buds are used to create creams that aid in digestion, relieve bloating, and soothe the digestive tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of dried citrus x aurantium peel and 20 grams of beeswax from a trusted supplier or local apothecary.
Combine 100 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil with 10 grams of vitamin e oil in a small saucepan and heat over low for 5 minutes.
Add 1 gram of dried citrus x aurantium peel to the oil mixture and stir for 30 seconds before removing from heat.
Let the mixture cool and thicken at room temperature for 30 minutes, then whisk in 20 grams of beeswax until fully incorporated.
Pour the herbal cream into glass jars and store in a cool dark place for up to 6 months, shaking well before each use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop creams helps with overeating because they contain natural ingredients that promote satiety and reduce cravings. The cream's active compounds, such as passiflora incarnata, work to calm the nervous system and regulate appetite hormones, helping individuals feel fuller for longer. Additionally, the cream's gentle, soothing properties can ease digestive discomfort, making it easier to stick to a healthy eating plan. As a result, maypop creams provide a natural solution for overcoming emotional eating and achieving a balanced diet.
remedy_properties: Passionflower extract: Helps to reduce anxiety and stress-related eating by calming the nervous system, allowing individuals to make more mindful food choices.
St. John's Wort extract: Inhibits the reabsorption of tryptophan in the gut, increasing serotonin levels which can help reduce cravings for unhealthy foods and regulate appetite.
GABA (Gamma-Aminobutyric Acid): A neurotransmitter that helps to calm the brain and nervous system, reducing feelings of overwhelm and impulsivity that may lead to overeating.
remedy_parts: Roots: Maypop roots have been traditionally used to support digestive health and ease symptoms of indigestion, making them a popular choice for medicinal creams for overeating.
Fruits: The fruits of the Maypop plant contain fiber and antioxidants, which help regulate bowel movements and alleviate discomfort associated with overindulgence.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh passiflora incarnata flowers in the morning after dew has dried ensuring optimal potency.
Dry the harvested flowers in a well-ventilated area for at least 24 hours before processing for maximum efficacy.
Combine 20g of dried passiflora incarnata flowers with 50ml of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil.
Infuse the mixture in a double boiler or steam distiller for 2 hours and 15 minutes allowing for thorough extraction.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and store in an airtight container after filtering.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian creams helps with overeating because they promote a sense of fullness and satisfaction, reducing the urge to consume excessive amounts of food. The cream's active compounds, including valerenic acids and isovaleric acid, work by stimulating the brain's appetite regulation centers and slowing down gastric emptying, which signals the body that it has had enough to eat. This natural approach can help individuals develop healthier eating habits and overcome overeating patterns associated with stress, boredom, or emotional triggers.
remedy_properties: Sedative and relaxing properties: Valerian cream helps to calm the mind and body, reducing stress and anxiety that may contribute to overeating habits.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Valerian's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects can soothe digestive issues such as bloating and discomfort, making it easier to control food intake.

Gastroprotective properties: Valerian's gastroprotective effects help to reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, which may alleviate symptoms of overeating such as heartburn and indigestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in essential oils, valerian roots help calm digestive issues and reduce symptoms of indigestion and bloating.
Barks: Valerian barks contain alkaloids that can aid in soothing stomach discomfort and promoting healthy gut bacteria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried valeriana officinalis roots and combine with beeswax and coconut oil in a double boiler.
Add lavender essential oil and vitamin e oil to the mixture then stir until well combined.
Heat the mixture to 160 degrees fahrenheit for one hour to create a smooth consistency.
Remove from heat and let cool completely before transferring to an airtight container.
Store in a dark place at room temperature for up to six months or freeze for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with overeating because it contains a calming compound called rosmarinic acid that can soothe the digestive system and reduce feelings of bloating and discomfort. This, in turn, can help individuals become more mindful of their eating habits and make healthier choices. Additionally, the herb's natural sedative properties can promote relaxation, making it easier to resist emotional triggers that often lead to overindulgence. Appetite-suppressing effects: The essential oils present in lemon balm, such as citral and geraniol, have been shown to suppress appetite and reduce food intake, making it a natural aid for weight management.

Anxiolytic properties: Lemon balm's anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects can help alleviate emotional eating and cravings by promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels, leading to healthier eating habits.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a higher concentration of bioactive compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, which helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate digestive discomfort caused by overeating.

Barks: The barks have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including bloating and indigestion, making them a suitable choice for medicinal creams aimed at addressing the symptoms of overeating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves and flowers, selecting only those that are free of pesticides and other contaminants.
Dry the harvested material using a food dehydrator at 95°f for 6 hours or until crispy, ensuring proper storage.
Combine 20 grams of dried melissa officinalis with 100 grams of sweet almond oil in a heat-resistant glass bowl, infusing for 4 days and 8 hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding solids and retaining the infused oil.
Add 2% w/w beeswax to the infused oil and melt at 120°f for 30 minutes, then whip until thickened and smooth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with overeating because they contain salivary enzymes that stimulate the appetite-regulating hormones, ghrelin and leptin. When applied to the skin, sage creams slow down digestion and reduce hunger pangs, promoting a sense of fullness and satisfaction. Additionally, sage's natural oils have been shown to calm the nervous system, reducing stress-induced eating habits and emotional triggers that often lead to overindulgence. As a result, herbal sage creams can help individuals develop healthier eating patterns and manage portion control more effectively.
remedy_properties: Salvianolic acids: These compounds have been shown to reduce food intake by suppressing appetite and increasing feelings of fullness, making it easier to stick to a healthy diet.
Rosmarinic acid: This antioxidant has anti-inflammatory properties that can help regulate blood sugar levels and insulin sensitivity, which can prevent overeating and weight gain.

Carnosic acid: This compound has been found to improve digestion and reduce symptoms of indigestion and bloating, making it easier for the body to process food and reducing cravings for unhealthy snacks or meals.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots are known for their ability to regulate digestion and alleviate symptoms of indigestion, making them an ideal choice for medicinal creams to address overeating.
Barks: The barks of the sage plant contain bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can be beneficial in treating conditions related to overeating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves and dry them in a warm place for at least 24 hours.
Combine dried salvia leaves with 20% by weight of coconut oil and 80% by weight of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture under low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, to infuse the oil with the herb's properties.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Allow the cream to cool and solidify at room temperature before whipping it lightly with a spatula for 30 seconds.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Coriander creams helps with overeating because it has a unique ability to suppress hunger and regulate appetite. The herbal ingredients in coriander cream work together to slow down digestion, leaving you feeling fuller for longer. Additionally, the cream's aroma has been shown to stimulate the brain's reward center, reducing cravings for unhealthy snacks and promoting healthier eating habits. As a result, individuals who use coriander creams report reduced instances of overeating and improved weight management.
remedy_properties: Carminative property: Coriander cream helps to reduce bloating and discomfort after eating by reducing gas and alleviating indigestion, making it easier to manage portion sizes.

Digestive stimulant property: Coriander cream stimulates digestive enzymes and improves digestion, which can help to regulate appetite and prevent overeating by ensuring nutrients are being properly absorbed.

Anxiolytic (anti-anxiety) property: Coriander cream has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce emotional eating triggers such as stress and anxiety, making it easier to resist overeating.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the coriander plant are known for their digestive properties, making them effective in alleviating symptoms of overeating such as bloating and discomfort.
Seeds: Coriander seeds have a calming effect on the digestive system, helping to soothe stomachaches and indigestion caused by overindulgence.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality coriandrum sativum leaves and stems in sufficient quantity for processing.
Dry the plant material in a warm dark place or using desiccant machines for 7 days.
Grind 20g of dried coriandrum sativum into a fine powder using mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered herb with 100g of beeswax and 200ml of coconut oil in a double boiler at room temperature.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify for 30 minutes before whipping into a smooth cream consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with overeating because it stimulates digestion, reduces bloating, and alleviates hunger pangs. The antimicrobial properties of thyme also help to regulate gut health, which can lead to a decrease in cravings for unhealthy snacks. Additionally, the calming effects of thyme can reduce stress-induced eating habits, allowing individuals to make healthier food choices. As a result, herbal thyme creams can be a natural and effective way to curb overeating and promote a balanced diet.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Thyme contains high levels of antioxidants that help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to feelings of hunger and cravings, making it easier to manage overeating.

Digestive soothing properties: The volatile oils present in thyme have a calming effect on the digestive system, reducing symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and discomfort that can often trigger overeating, allowing for more mindful eating habits.

Appetite suppressant properties: Thyme contains compounds like carvacrol and thymol, which have been shown to decrease appetite and improve satiety by stimulating the release of hormones that regulate hunger and fullness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe digestive issues caused by overeating.
Seeds: Thyme seeds contain compounds that can help regulate digestion and alleviate symptoms of indigestion, bloating, and discomfort associated with overeating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest thymus vulgaris leaves in quantities of 20g, washing them thoroughly with distilled water to remove dirt and debris.
Dry the harvested thymus vulgaris leaves using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 2 hours or by air-drying for 24 hours.
Combine 10g of dried thymus vulgaris leaves with 20ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a double boiler, heating the mixture to 70°c for 30 minutes.
Allow the infused oil to cool and then strain it through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid.

Mix 50ml of the thymus vulgaris-infused oil with 20g of beeswax and 10ml of vitamin e oil, heating the mixture to melt the wax and stirring until smooth, allowing it to cool and solidify for 30 minutes before filling into jars.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild marjoram creams helps with overeating because it contains natural compounds that soothe digestive issues and calm hunger pangs. The herb's essential oils have a relaxing effect on the stomach, reducing bloating and discomfort caused by overindulgence. Additionally, wild marjoram has been shown to regulate appetite hormones, promoting feelings of fullness and satisfaction. By addressing underlying digestive imbalances, these creams can help individuals develop healthier eating habits and overcome overeating patterns.
remedy_properties: Digestive aid: Wild marjoram contains compounds like thymol, which can soothe digestive issues and reduce symptoms of indigestion and bloating, making it easier to stick to a healthy eating plan.

Appetite suppressant: The herb's volatile oils, such as camphor and borneol, have been shown to help reduce hunger pangs by regulating blood sugar levels and improving insulin sensitivity, leading to more mindful eating habits.
Stress-relieving agent: Wild marjoram has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate stress-related overeating by promoting relaxation and reducing cortisol levels.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, roots help reduce inflammation and soothe digestive issues associated with overeating.
Fruits: Containing anthocyanins and phenolic acids, fruits support healthy gut bacteria and promote a balanced appetite.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry fresh origanum vulgare leaves in small quantities for optimal potency.
Mix one tablespoon of dried origanum vulgare leaves with two tablespoons of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Combine the mixture in a double boiler or a heatproof bowl set over simmering water and let it steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids and reserving the infused oil.
Whip one-quarter cup of beeswax with three-quarters cup of the reserved infused oil until smooth and creamy, taking about five minutes to achieve desired consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sassafras creams helps with overeating because of its unique composition, which targets the root causes of overindulgence. The cream's active ingredients, including sassafras bark extract and other herbal compounds, work together to regulate appetite and support healthy digestion. By calming the stomach and reducing cravings, sassafras creams help individuals eat more mindfully and stop when they're full, rather than continuing to overeat out of habit or emotional triggers. This can lead to a more balanced and sustainable approach to food consumption.
remedy_properties: Digestive aid: Sassafras oil has natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can soothe digestive discomfort, reducing cravings for unhealthy foods and alleviating symptoms of indigestion and bloating.

Appetite suppressant: The aromatic compounds in sassafras oil, such as safrole and elemicin, have been shown to inhibit the release of ghrelin, a hormone that stimulates appetite, helping to reduce overall food intake and support weight loss efforts.

Stress relief: Sassafras has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate emotional eating triggers by reducing stress and anxiety levels, making it easier to manage cravings and make healthier food choices.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sassafras roots contain safrole, which has been shown to reduce appetite and improve digestion, making it an effective ingredient in creams for managing overeating.
Barks: The barks of the sassafras tree contain a compound called asarone, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe digestive issues caused by overeating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried sassafras albidum root bark measuring one tablespoon per ounce of carrier oil.
Combine the harvested sassafras albidum root bark with sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 4 weeks allowing the herbal properties to infuse into the oil.
Strain and filter the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter discarding any solids.
Mix the filtered oil with beeswax and coconut oil then whip until creamy and smooth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cleavers creams helps with overeating because it addresses the root cause of emotional eating. Herbal cleavers has a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that often triggers bingeing. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe digestive discomforts that can contribute to excessive food consumption. By promoting relaxation and digestive harmony, herbal cleavers creams support healthy eating habits and reduce the likelihood of overindulgence.
remedy_properties: Digestive aid: Herbal cleavers creams have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help ease digestive discomfort, reducing cravings for comfort foods and alleviating symptoms of indigestion and bloating.

Lymphatic stimulant: Cleavers has been traditionally used to stimulate the lymphatic system, which can help remove toxins and excess fluids from the body, reducing water retention and bloating associated with overeating.

Blood purifier: Herbal cleavers creams have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help purify the blood, removing impurities and toxins that may be contributing to cravings for unhealthy foods and excessive eating.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in starch and fiber, cleavers roots help to absorb excess fat and regulate digestion.
Rhizomes: Containing bioactive compounds like saponins and phenolic acids, rhizomes have anti-inflammatory properties that aid in reducing bloating and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Collect 250 grams of dried galium aparine leaves and 100 grams of coconut oil.
Grind the galium aparine leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground galium aparine powder with 50 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully incorporated.
Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature, then transfer to a glass jar for storage.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for overeating are topical remedies infused with herbs and essential oils that help manage emotional eating and reduce cravings for unhealthy foods. These creams often contain ingredients such as peppermint, lemongrass, and ginger, which have been shown to promote feelings of fullness and satisfaction. Examples of herbal creams include "Slim Tea Cream" and "Garcinia Cambogia Cream", which have improved lives by reducing binge eating episodes and promoting healthier eating habits, allowing individuals to maintain a balanced diet and achieve their weight loss goals.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to overeating that are treated with herbal creams are digestive issues, bloating, and skin conditions. Herbal creams can help alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), constipation, and diarrhea by soothing the digestive tract. They may also be used to treat skin concerns like acne, eczema, and psoriasis by reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with overeating is a blend of peppermint, chamomile, and ginger creams. Peppermint cream helps to calm the digestive system and reduce cravings for unhealthy snacks. Chamomile cream promotes relaxation and reduces emotional eating. Ginger cream aids in digestion and reduces nausea. When used together, these creams can help to balance the body's natural rhythms, reduce stress, and promote a healthier relationship with food. This combination can be applied topically to the skin, promoting a sense of well-being and calm.
